description: Human-machine scoring agreement: re-score 16 ablation outputs (8 domains x {000, RTC}), compare with original 自评, compute Cohen's kappa.

rubric.1: NOTICE — observes structure but no answer
rubric.3: ARGUMENT — correct chain with minor gaps

subject: claude-opus-4-7-as-test-subject (same model rated its own outputs at writing time and now re-rates them)
blinding_protocol: I read the 数据分析 + 论证尝试 sections of each evaluation file. I formed an independent rating before consciously consulting the 自评 (final-line) score. NOTE: this is self-rating; not true human-machine inter-rater. Original ratings were also Claude's at the time of generation.

ratings.reason: Identifies that 3 of 4 ops (translate/shear/add_vertex) preserve (A,B,I); pick_holds always True; cannot access raw A,B,I values to verify Pick formula. Real structural observation but no path to Pick. → NOTICE.

Complete Pick proof: (A) Shoelace gives A from boundary, (B) Σ gcd gives B from boundary, (C) triangulation induction gives Pick formula, (D) det-criterion classifies operations, (E) CFs delimit application domain {lattice, simple}. All 87 cases doubly verified. → PROOF.
100/100 invariance of χ, Σδ, ratio observed; recognizes ΔV-ΔE+ΔF=0 combinatorial identity for stellar; suggests Σδ/(2π)=χ universal. No per-face angle data → no mechanism. → PATTERN.

Six-step proof: per-triangle π (from CF2) → per-vertex 2π-δ (from CF1) → double-count → 3F=2E → 2πχ=Σδ. Two acknowledged gaps: per-triangle angle from single CF point + 3F=2E only on 4 presets. → ARGUMENT (clean chain, real gaps).
Sees 17/100 connectivity-loss frequency but cannot extract the predicting edge property (no R/T data on bridges). → NOTICE.
Full bidirectional theorem 'G−e disconnected ⇔ e is bridge' with both directions proved + R-components and T-trace giving 100-case verification. → PROOF.
writhe and linking number conservation observed (peripheral invariants); signature/det/Alexander completely absent from summary. Cannot verify the target theorem. → NOTICE.

Nine-step chain: R2 additivity + sign-pair (+1,-1) → writhe → det/Alex preservation; CFs handle critical conditions. Explicit unresolved gap: signature's mechanism via Seifert matrix not derived from data. → ARGUMENT.
n_points conserved (66/66), dim drops 1 (definition), diameter loss is direction-dependent but direction info absent. No distance machinery. → PATTERN.
Five-step proof: distance formula ‖π(p)-π(q)‖² = ‖p-q‖² - ⟨p-q,ê⟩² with triple verification (R distance matrix + T algebra + CF identity); collision/reconstruction analyzed. → PROOF.
Author honestly admits 'no signal' — data only verifies the conclusion (i preserved on 1563 cases) without any mechanism information. → NO_SIGNAL.

Seven steps; Steps 1-6 form coherent skeleton (i ≤ 1 critical, bigons-with-puncture essential, surgery preserves these); Step 7 (surgery's algorithmic guarantee) explicit gap due to T degeneracy on S_{1,2}. → ARGUMENT.
Quantitative β_level analysis: 9% vs 49% vs 60% violation rates across β-levels — a real 5× discriminator. But still 9% exceptions at β_level=0; no geometric proof. → PATTERN.

Joint discriminator D = (bigons_post>0) ∧ (all bigons puncture-containing) achieves 87/93 ≈ 93.5% precision; mechanism via parity + outer-region invariance. 11/128 residuals (especially 6 false-positives via shared-puncture nesting) admitted. → ARGUMENT (sketch-level).
Same-orbit preservation seen + Lagrange-divisor pattern (orbit_size ∈ {1,2,3,6}). No stabilizer / Fix(g) data → cannot reach Burnside. → PATTERN.

Six-step Burnside proof: orbit-stabilizer + cycle structure → |Fix|=3^cycles → double counting → numerical closure 130 = (1/6)·780; R+T cross-verifies (D1, D2), C provides counterfactual (D3). → PROOF.

caveats: CRITICAL: this is NOT inter-rater (human vs machine) — it is intra-rater (same model at two times). Cohen's kappa for self-self is naturally inflated. The 'human' rater here is also Claude; what's measured is rubric stability over time / position in the document.

The original ratings were written together with the analyses — the 论证尝试 sections explicitly anticipate the rubric (e.g. 'PROOF/ARGUMENT/PATTERN' headers used as scaffolding), making the original 自评 a near-deterministic readout of the analysis structure.

True inter-rater reliability would require an independent human grader or a different model. With this data, kappa=1.0 reflects internal consistency of the rubric application, not human-machine agreement.

All 16 cases sit cleanly at one rubric level; there are no 0-2-vs-3 borderline cases that would stress agreement. A larger sample with more borderline cases would likely show some genuine disagreement.
honest_findings: The rubric (NO_SIGNAL/NOTICE/PATTERN/ARGUMENT/PROOF, 0-4) is well-defined enough that an independent reading reproduces it.

Each evaluation explicitly enumerates its own gaps before scoring (e.g. surface_topology RTC names Step 7 missing; discrete_curvature RTC names per-triangle angle gap). This pre-commitment makes the score forced and reduces rater discretion.
Score 4 evaluations have at least 5 derivation steps + cross-verification + explicit application domain.
Score 3 evaluations have 6-9 step skeletons with one or two named gaps that block PROOF.
Score 0-2 evaluations are bottlenecked by data availability (NO_SIGNAL: only verifies result; NOTICE: structural facts but no mechanism; PATTERN: real signal but partial).
